name: Corbenic
description: Corbenic is the mystical castle where the Holy Grail is kept in Arthurian legend. It is often described as a magnificent, yet perilous, place, surrounded by strange enchantments and guarded by powerful beings. The castle is the home of the Fisher King, who is wounded and can only be healed by a knight worthy of the Grail. Corbenic is not just a physical location but also a spiritual one, representing the quest for spiritual enlightenment and the challenges one must overcome to achieve it. The Grail's presence makes it a nexus of divine power and a focal point for the Arthurian romances, drawing knights from across the land in search of its blessings. The castle's appearance and accessibility often fluctuate depending on the worthiness of the seeker, reflecting the inner journey required to attain spiritual grace. It is a place of trials, temptations, and ultimate redemption, embodying the core themes of Arthurian mythology.
subtitle: The Castle of the Holy Grail

keyMyths.title: The Grail Quest
keyMyths.description: Knights journey to Corbenic seeking the Holy Grail, facing numerous trials to prove their worthiness. Only the purest of heart can succeed in the quest and heal the Fisher King.

keyMyths.title: The Fisher King's Wound
keyMyths.description: The Fisher King suffers from a mysterious wound that can only be healed by a question about the Grail. His suffering reflects the barrenness of the land and the spiritual malaise of the kingdom.
keyMyths.source: Perceval, the Story of the Grail

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian tales, featuring Corbenic as the location of the Holy Grail.
sources.text: A comprehensive compilation of Arthurian tales, featuring Corbenic as the location of the Holy Grail.

sources.author: Chrétien de Troyes
sources.work: Perceval, the Story of the Grail
sources.description: An unfinished romance that introduces the Grail and the Fisher King, setting the stage for the Corbenic mythos.
sources.text: An unfinished romance that introduces the Grail and the Fisher King, setting the stage for the Corbenic mythos.
